title: Making A Large Island
hints: Consider the island sizes if no flip occurred. How can you efficiently identify which cell belongs to which island?
Label each existing island with a unique ID and store its size. Then, for every 0, check its neighbors' IDs to calculate the potential new island size.
Iterate through all 0s. Sum the sizes of unique neighboring islands plus one. Track the maximum found, handling the edge case where no 0 exists separately.
